heading: Financial responsibility
label: 2716 Financial responsibility

CodificationIn subsec. (b)(1), “” substituted for “section 4197 of the Revised Statutes of the United States” on authority of , , , which Act enacted , Shipping.section 60105 of title 46Pub. L. 109–304, § 18(c)120 Stat. 1709section 60105 of Title 46Oct. 6, 2006

Amendments2025—Subsecs. (d) to (i). redesignated subsecs. (e) to (i) as (d) to (h), respectively, and, in subsec. (e)(1)(B) as redesignated, substituted “subsection (d)” for “subsection (e)”.Pub. L. 119–602010—Subsec. (a)(3). added par. (3).Pub. L. 111–2811996—Subsec. (c)(1). , reenacted heading without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Except as provided in paragraph (2), each responsible party with respect to an offshore facility shall establish and maintain evidence of financial responsibility of $150,000,000 to meet the amount of liability to which the responsible party could be subjected under in a case in which the responsible party would be entitled to limit liability under that section. In a case in which a person is the responsible party for more than one facility subject to this subsection, evidence of financial responsibility need be established only to meet the maximum liability applicable to the facility having the greatest maximum liability.”Pub. L. 104–324, § 1125(a)(1)section 2704(a) of this titleSubsec. (f). , reenacted heading without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Any claim for which liability may be established under may be asserted directly against any guarantor providing evidence of financial responsibility for a responsible party liable under that section for removal costs and damages to which the claim pertains. In defending against such a claim, the guarantor may invoke (1) all rights and defenses which would be available to the responsible party under this Act, (2) any defense authorized under subsection (e), and (3) the defense that the incident was caused by the willful misconduct of the responsible party. The guarantor may not invoke any other defense that might be available in proceedings brought by the responsible party against the guarantor.”Pub. L. 104–324, § 1125(a)(2)section 2702 of this titleSubsec. (g). , reenacted heading without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Nothing in this Act shall impose liability with respect to an incident on any guarantor for damages or removal costs which exceed, in the aggregate, the amount of financial responsibility required under this Act which that guarantor has provided for a responsible party.”Pub. L. 104–324, § 1125(a)(3)1995—Subsec. (a). substituted “the responsible party could be subjected under section 2704(a) or (d) of this title” for “, in the case of a tank vessel, the responsible party could be subject under section 2704(a)(1) or (d) of this title, or to which, in the case of any other vessel, the responsible party could be subjected under section 2704(a)(2) or (d) of this title”.Pub. L. 104–55

Delegation of FunctionsSpecific functions of President under subsec. (e) of this section delegated to Secretary of the Interior and Secretary of the Department in which the Coast Guard is operating by section 5(a) of Ex. Ord. No. 12777, , 56 F.R. 54764, as amended, set out as a note under .Oct. 18, 1991section 1321 of this title

children.text: any vessel over 300 gross tons (except a non-self-propelled vessel that does not carry oil as cargo or fuel) using any place subject to the jurisdiction of the United States;
any vessel using the waters of the exclusive economic zone to transship or lighter oil destined for a place subject to the jurisdiction of the United States; or
any tank vessel over 100 gross tons using any place subject to the jurisdiction of the United States;

children.heading: Withholding clearance
children.label: 1 Withholding clearance
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall withhold or revoke the clearance required by of any vessel subject to this section that does not have the evidence of financial responsibility required for the vessel under this section.section 60105 of title 46

children.heading: Denying entry to or detaining vessels
children.label: 2 Denying entry to or detaining vessels
A
children.text: deny entry to any vessel to any place in the United States, or to the navigable waters, or

children.label: B
children.text: detain at the place,

children.heading: Seizure of vessel
children.label: 3 Seizure of vessel
children.text: Any vessel subject to the requirements of this section which is found in the navigable waters without the necessary evidence of financial responsibility for the vessel shall be subject to seizure by and forfeiture to the United States.

children.heading: Evidence of financial responsibility required
children.label: A Evidence of financial responsibility required
I
children.text: is located seaward of the line of ordinary low water along that portion of the coast that is in direct contact with the open sea and the line marking the seaward limit of inland waters; or

children.label: II
children.text: is located in coastal inland waters, such as bays or estuaries, seaward of the line of ordinary low water along that portion of the coast that is not in direct contact with the open sea;

children.label: ii
children.text: is used for exploring for, drilling for, producing, or transporting oil from facilities engaged in oil exploration, drilling, or production; and

children.label: iii
children.text: has a worst-case oil spill discharge potential of more than 1,000 barrels of oil (or a lesser amount if the President determines that the risks posed by such facility justify it),

children.label: i
children.text: $35,000,000 for an offshore facility located seaward of the seaward boundary of a State; or

children.label: ii
children.text: $10,000,000 for an offshore facility located landward of the seaward boundary of a State.

children.heading: Greater amount
children.label: C Greater amount
children.text: If the President determines that an amount of financial responsibility for a responsible party greater than the amount required by subparagraph (B) is justified based on the relative operational, environmental, human health, and other risks posed by the quantity or quality of oil that is explored for, drilled for, produced, or transported by the responsible party, the evidence of financial responsibility required shall be for an amount determined by the President not exceeding $150,000,000.

children.heading: Multiple facilities
children.label: D Multiple facilities
children.text: In a case in which a person is a responsible party for more than one facility subject to this subsection, evidence of financial responsibility need be established only to meet the amount applicable to the facility having the greatest financial responsibility requirement under this subsection.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: E Definition
children.text: For the purpose of this paragraph, the seaward boundary of a State shall be determined in accordance with .section 1301(b) of title 43

children.heading: Deepwater ports
children.label: 2 Deepwater ports
children.text: Each responsible party with respect to a deepwater port shall establish and maintain evidence of financial responsibility sufficient to meet the maximum amount of liability to which the responsible party could be subjected under in a case where the responsible party would be entitled to limit liability under that section. If the Secretary exercises the authority under to lower the limit of liability for deepwater ports, the responsible party shall establish and maintain evidence of financial responsibility sufficient to meet the maximum amount of liability so established. In a case in which a person is the responsible party for more than one deepwater port, evidence of financial responsibility need be established only to meet the maximum liability applicable to the deepwater port having the greatest maximum liability.section 2704(a) of this titlesection 2704(d)(2) of this title

children.heading: Methods of financial responsibility
children.label: d Methods of financial responsibility
children.text: Financial responsibility under this section may be established by any one, or by any combination, of the following methods which the Secretary (in the case of a vessel) or the President (in the case of a facility) determines to be acceptable: evidence of insurance, surety bond, guarantee, letter of credit, qualification as a self-insurer, or other evidence of financial responsibility. Any bond filed shall be issued by a bonding company authorized to do business in the United States. In promulgating requirements under this section, the Secretary or the President, as appropriate, may specify policy or other contractual terms, conditions, or defenses which are necessary, or which are unacceptable, in establishing evidence of financial responsibility to effectuate the purposes of this Act.

children.label: A
children.text: all rights and defenses which would be available to the responsible party under this Act;

children.label: B
children.text: any defense authorized under subsection (d); and

children.label: C
children.text: the defense that the incident was caused by the willful misconduct of the responsible party.

children.label: A
children.text: the responsible party for whom evidence of financial responsibility has been provided has denied or failed to pay a claim under this Act on the basis of being insolvent, as defined under , and applying generally accepted accounting principles;section 101(32) of title 11

children.label: B
children.text: the responsible party for whom evidence of financial responsibility has been provided has filed a petition for bankruptcy under title 11; or

children.label: C
children.text: the claim is asserted by the United States for removal costs and damages or for compensation paid by the Fund under this Act, including costs incurred by the Fund for processing compensation claims.

children.heading: Rulemaking authority
children.label: 3 Rulemaking authority
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the President shall promulgate regulations to establish a process for implementing paragraph (2) in a manner that will allow for the orderly and expeditious presentation and resolution of claims and effectuate the purposes of this Act.October 19, 1996

children.heading: Limitation on guarantor’s liability
children.label: f Limitation on guarantor’s liability
children.text: Nothing in this Act shall impose liability with respect to an incident on any guarantor for damages or removal costs which exceed, in the aggregate, the amount of financial responsibility which that guarantor has provided for a responsible party pursuant to this section. The total liability of the guarantor on direct action for claims brought under this Act with respect to an incident shall be limited to that amount.

children.heading: Continuation of regulations
children.label: g Continuation of regulations
children.text: Any regulation relating to financial responsibility, which has been issued pursuant to any provision of law repealed or superseded by this Act, and which is in effect on the date immediately preceding the effective date of this Act, is deemed and shall be construed to be a regulation issued pursuant to this section. Such a regulation shall remain in full force and effect unless and until superseded by a new regulation issued under this section.

children.heading: Unified certificate
children.label: h Unified certificate
children.text: The Secretary may issue a single unified certificate of financial responsibility for purposes of this Act and any other law.
